We present a novel approach to probing the space-time geometry using the statistical properties of the observed structures in the universe. Using the redshift-temperature relation of the cosmic microwave background (CMB) and the distortion information from the Integrated Sachs-Wolfe (ISW) effect, we construct the so-called "entropy-temperature" plane. We show that the cosmological constant, which is believed to drive the current expansion of the universe, affects the theoretical predictions in this plane in a distinct manner from various dark energy candidates. As an example, we apply our method to the latest observed Sloan Digital Sky Survey (SDSS) data and put a tight constraint on the space-time curvature tensor in the universe. Using the full galaxy sample, our analysis improves the constraint on the non-flat universe from the previous result by Anderson et al. (2013) by more than a factor of two. We forecast that the forthcoming results from the completed SDSS-III BOSS survey will further improve the constraint on the curvature parameters.

The dataset proposed in the paper is based on HOL, and the main purpose of the dataset is to help develop new machine learning-based theorem-proving strategies. The dataset is also made publicly available under the BSD license. We propose various machine learning tasks that can be performed on this dataset, and discuss their significance for theorem proving. The results of our baseline models show the promise of applying machine learning to HOL theorem proving. I think this peer review is interesting and worthy to be published. This is because the proposed machine learning tasks are significant for the development of theorem proving strategies, and the baseline models can perform the proposed tasks well. These facts have not been shown in previous works.I recommend to publish this paper. Yixin Wu Comments: I think this paper is an important step for using machine learning to solve logic problems. The authors construct a large HOL dataset and design several tasks which are significant for theorem proving, and benchmark baseline models for performing the tasks. The results show the promise of applying machine learning to solve logic problems. However, I have some comments as follows: 1. There is no human component in the dataset, so the models trained on the dataset may overfit to the data, and may not perform well on new datasets. For example, the baseline model may perform well on the classification task, but may perform badly on regression task. 2. The task itself is interesting, but there are some previous works that are similar to this task. For example, the model can be used to generate solutions to puzzles in the HOL book. The dataset is also made publicly available under an open source license. So the model can be downloaded and tried out. I think that the human-component and the similarity to previous works may limit the usage of the paper, but the interesting machine learning part is still relevant. Thank you for sharing the useful data set. I am willing to help you promote it by putting a link to your website in CHI and posting a brief mention on the Facebook group for HOL. Best, Yixin Wu Additional comments: Thank you for the positive comments and suggestions. We have taken these into consideration and have tried to emphasize the human-component in the paper. As for the similarity to previous works, we have added the explanation to the beginning of the paper. Reference: https://ai.tencent.com/product/ HolStep Please also check the license under which the dataset is made publicly available. The license does not allow others to directly use the dataset for commercial purpose. This is because the dataset is made for developing new strategies and using the dataset may lead to improvement of the strategies. The dataset is made publicly available under the BSD license, which allows others to use the dataset for commercial purposes. There are other similar dataset which are also under the BSD license. For example, the HOL4X datasets from Max Planck Institute for Informatics. However, the HOL4X datasets only contains some primitive logical rules, such as propositional tautology and contradiction. It is not suitable for machine learning-based theorem proving. On the other hand, the HolStep dataset is more complicated. There are some basic logical rules such as K3 and k4, but also some advanced heuristics such as resolution and LJ. The benchmarked models also perform much better on the HolStep dataset than on the HOL4X dataset. Please also note that the dataset is also not fully automatic in the sense that we have manually removed some obviously invalid proofs from the dataset. The models trained on the dataset can be used to generate new proofs. However, the new proofs have to be manually inspected. The new dataset is made publicly available under the BSD license. We have also removed invalid proofs from the dataset. Thanks for the suggestion. We have updated the manuscript accordingly. We have also removed the sentences related to the license. We think that the new dataset can be used for multiple research purposes, and not just for developing new strategies. It is interesting to see the performance of the baseline models. We also performed the new experiments. The new baseline models perform much worse than the first set of models (with the previous median loss). The average loss is still slightly better than the first set of models. These results confirm our previous suspicion. However, I think that the new dataset does not have the same significance as the previous dataset. The new dataset is only a subset of the previous dataset. The new dataset has already been used to train the new baseline models. It is not very interesting to see how the new models perform on the new dataset, because the new dataset is already known. On the other hand, the previous dataset may contain new ideas, which may be difficult to get

Using a compilation of 185 type Ia supernova peak-magnitude versus rise time measurements, we present a systematic analysis of the diversity among these supernovae in relation to their decline rate-corrected rise times (sink times). We show that a physical model with a single rise time distribution cannot describe the data. A two-component decay model, with a small fraction (~15%) of supernovae exhibiting fast rise times (rise time < 15 days) and the majority (~85%) of supernovae exhibiting rise times in the range 15 - 40 days, is preferred over a single-component model at a significance of 4.3 standard deviations. The fast-rise-time supernovae have more negative peak magnitudes, slower decline rates, and longer rise times than those with normal rise times, indicating that the fast-rise-time supernovae are an intrinsically different subset of supernovae. These results suggest that variations in the physical properties of type Ia supernovae are evident even at the earliest stages of their explosion.

The paper is related to exploring layer attribute in deep neural networks, and the main content is well covered. The method can be used for visualizations, assessment of feature invariances and general synthesis of adversarial examples, and they are all covered in the paper. My only concern is that the connection between these ideas and their applications are not that clearly shown, and this might be a reason that the paper is still in review. absorption layer attribute in deep neural networks can be used for visualizations assessment of feature invariances general synthesis of adversarial examples constitute a general way of producing a vast amount of diverse adversarial examples improve the robustness of machine learning models Reviewer #1 Beauty Knowledgeability Room for Improvement In my opinion, the biggest flaw in the paper is that it lacks a concrete use for the proposed technique. There is no explanation of why the layer attribute is important or interesting, and therefore the technique could be considered a nice abstraction without much practical application. I assume that this is because the paper was originally intended for a broader audience, but I think that a more concrete use-case would greatly enhance the impact of the paper. For example, the authors could apply their technique to assess the vulnerabilities of real-world machine learning models, likeComputer vision models that detect fraudulent transactions (e.g., a picture of a forged check) or diagnose disease (a medical image). By identifying the layers responsible for particular invariances in these models, it might be possible to break the models’ invariances and use adversarial examples to force them to make incorrect predictions. In this way, the layer attribute could be used as a way of improving the robustness of these models.

Choose your grapes, aiming for fresh, sweet, and flavorful berries. Pick the grapes off the bunch and wash them off. Put the grapes in a large pot and cover with 2-1/2 cups of water. Bring the berries and water to a boil for 10 minutes, stirring regularly, covered. Place the grapes into a jelly bag so that the juice drains out into a jar or pot below. Don't add water. Add the pectin and bring the juice to a full boil once more. Add the 2-1/2 cups of sugar, bringing the juice back to a boil. Once the sugar is all dissolved, cut the heat and skim any foam off the top. Pour the hot liquid immediately into the hot canning jars. Start boiling a large pot of water, enough to cover your cans. Sterilize your jars and lids before you begin cooking. Fill the jars with jelly and put the clean lids and rings on. Gently place the closed jars in the boiling water for 5- 10 minutes. Let the jars cool overnight and serve when done. Check that they jelly was properly preserved by pushing the top down.

Extracurricular activities are important to a students' overall well-being and education. By requiring students to participate in at least one extracurricular activity, the school is helping to ensure that each student remains physically and socially active. Most students do not have the flexibility to attend sporting events, musical performances, or art exhibits, so this requirement gives them the opportunity to experience these activities. Additionally, students who participate in extracurricular activities are typically learning new things, whether it is languages they can speak, skills they can learn, or concepts they can understand. Finally, by engaging in extracurricular activities, students can get ready for the future by practicing problem-solving and critical thinking. They can also get exposed to different schools of thought and different perspectives, which helps them be more adaptable as an adult. Overall, requiring students to engage in at least one extracurricular activity is positive for students and the community. The students who participate in extracurricular activities actually enjoy them because they are a change of pace from their schoolwork. The activities help students be more physically and socially active, learn new things, and prepare for the future. On the other hand, there are some potential negative outcomes to requiring students to participate in extracurricular activities, such as administrative headaches and the risk that students put their commitments ahead of their schoolwork. However, the possible positive outcomes, such as students staying physically and socially active and learning new things, are worth the risk.

Modern wireless networks exhibit an ever-increasing demand for higher data rates. To meet this demand, wireless communication systems must leverage multi-antenna technology, which provides the potential for significant increase in spectral efficiency through spatial multiplexing. However, the practical implementation of such technology poses several significant challenges. Chief among these challenges is the understanding of the fundamental limits of such systems in the large-system limit. In this work, we derive such limits for a popular transmission scheme known as spatial multiplexing with tabular channel statistics. In particular, we characterize the diversity-multiplexing tradeoff for the case where the number of antennas at the transmitter and receiver grow large, but the ratio between them is constrained. We further characterize the impact of realistic channel estimation error on these limits. Our results demonstrate that even with channel estimation error, spatial multiplexing with a large number of antennas still enjoys the benefits of a diversity multiplexing tradeoff, albeit with reduced rate.

The Facial Action Coding System (FACS) is a method for measuring human facial movements. Through video surveillance and/or computer software, FACS coding can be used to quantify and describe human facial movements. By looking at a video recording of someone's face, for example, FACS coding can tell you what kinds of muscle movements were present, at what time, and in what sequence. The use of FACS in the classroom could help both students and teachers. For students, using FACS in the classroom would allow teachers to see when they are entering a zone where their emotions are out of balance, and provide feedback to help students adjust accordingly. Additionally, a student who is having a hard time focusing may be able to turn to the teacher and see that the facereader can see that the student is struggling, and the teacher could then help the student focus by, for example, walking over to the student or giving a verbal suggestion. For teachers, FACS coding would allow teachers to gather data on students' reactions to certain activities, which could help the teacher see what is or is not working. Also, FACS coding would allow teachers to see if a student's reaction to a lesson matches what was intended, and could help the teacher understand why that is the case. There are a lot of positives and negatives to using FACS in the classroom. For example, if FACS coding is not done carefully, it could help students who perform certain activities, such as mock courts, gain an unfair advantage over other students. Additionally, there are concerns that, because FACS coding is done through computer software, it can be easily skewed to show the population as a whole the way someone wants them to see it, rather than the reality of the situation. FACS coding has the potential to help both teachers and students, and although there are some concerns, the use of FACS in the classroom could be a huge advancement in the educational system.

In this paper we compare numerical relativity (NR) and post-Newtonian (PN) waveforms for neutron-star binaries with mass ratios up to 6:1. We build on recent publications that established effective methods to match the two waveform families at a specified post-merger frequency (known as the hereditary or spectrally truncated approximation). We show that accurate waveforms for physical properties, such as the radiated energy, can be obtained by matching the numerical and analytic waveforms at the effective center of mass (CCM) frame frequency. For nearly equal-mass systems, we find an excellent match with a maximum discrepancy of 3.3% in the total radiated energy, 1.3% in the phase, and 2.7% in the amplitude at twice the CCM frame frequency. For higher mass ratios, we find that total radiated energy estimates are accurate to 1.7%, and phase and amplitude corrections are accurate to 2.4%. We discuss optimizations to the matching procedure and present additional corrections to improve the waveform accuracy for a wider range of binary parameters. Finally, we provide the reader with a software tool that computes gravitational waveforms for neutron-star binaries using both the spectral truncation method and a novel hybrid approach that combines the strengths of both methods. The tool, named PyEFT, is publicly available and written in Python for general-purpose use.

How to Keep Your Family Safer and More Secure from Burglary and Fire 1. Always install quality locks on your doors and windows. To prevent burglars from entering sliding doors, place a pipe or broom handle in the bottom track of the door slide. Many burglaries happen when a house is empty. One of the most intrusive and dangerous forms of burglary occurs when somebody pretends to be a legitimate worker, for example from a utility company, and tricks their way into homes. Don't leave spare keys under doormats, potted plants or any other obvious outdoor location. Always keep your garage doors closed, even when you are home. When you go out in the evening, leave your lights on and shut the curtains. Develop the habit of performing a security review before leaving your home. Don't leave notes for service people or family members on the front door, mailbox or garage as these are green lights to potential burglars that you are not home. Ensure that your street address is clearly visible so that responding emergency personnel can easily identify your home. Install exterior security lighting to keep your home well lit on the outside. Keep your tools and ladders out of sight and securely locked up. Increasingly, burglars are breaking in to steal expensive cars. Don't leave empty boxes of new purchases outside your home that indicate new and valuable equipment that may be attractive to a potential burglar. 2. Never leave spare keys under doormats, potted plants or outside. 3. Keep your garage doors closed, even when you are home. 4. Don't leave notes for service people or family members on the front door, mailbox or garage as these are green lights to potential burglars that you are not home. 5. Ensure that your street address is clearly visible so that responding emergency personnel can easily identify your home. 6. Install exterior security lighting to keep your home well lit on the outside.

The problem the paper tries to solve is how to perform machine learning on time-series data, particularly if the data is non-i.i.d. orMissing Values. The solution the paper proposes is based on constructing an extended i.i.d. dataset by generating fresh data from a trained model. The fresh data is then used to train a new model which can be tested on held out data to measure performance. The main drawbacks of the proposed method is the requirement of a previously trained model and the requirement of lots of memory to train the generation model. mines and strengths: The main contribution of the paper is introducing the idea of extending an i.i.d. dataset to train a model that can handle non-i.i.d. data. The idea is quite intuitive and can be seen as generalizing the notion of independence to the domain of time-series data. The generation model used could potentially be made much more efficient by using specialized architectures such as GPT, but this would require further analysis. The Artifact Small Format Film Festival is an international film festival dedicated to irregular and experimental cinema. The festival was founded in 2006 in Rosario, Argentina and has been hosted in various cities around the world ever since, such as San Sebastian, Buenos Aires and Calcutta. The 10th edition of the festival will take place in San Sebastian, Spain from the 13th of August to the 4th of September 2020. The Artifact Festival was born of a need to have a local platform to present and promote films that did not fit into the commercial mainstream, and whose distribution was often insufficient for independent films. In its early days, the festival was hosted in underground venues, such as cinemas that did not have a proper address and old factories that could not accommodate large audiences. Over the years, the range of films shown at the Artifact Festival has broadened considerably, with sections devoted to obscure genres such as exploitation, horror, and torture films, as well as more innovative and artistic programming. As the festival has expanded, it has moved to larger and more prominent venues, including the San Sebastian Cinema Fundación and the Gala Cinemas. The Artifact Festival has several points of reference in Latin American and European independent cinema, both in terms of the works presented and the ethos of the festival. The festival is characterised by a non-hierarchical organisational structure, with a heavy emphasis on collaboration between participants and a rejection of commercialism. The programme is created collaboratively by the curatorial team, who invite suggestions from the film submissions. There is no jury, and the decisions of the selection committee are made transparent. This organisational structure is also reflected in the logistics of the festival. Admission to the festival is free and all screenings take place in the midst of the general public. The festival offers a number of amenities, including drinks and light refreshments, as well as events such as debates and discussions after the screenings. The Artifact Festival is truly a community event.

This question reminds me of a story about the feces being used as a local medicine in the eastern Europe. Let’s talk for a minute about germs. You and I both know that we are completely surrounded by bacteria and other microorganisms. These microorganisms live in and on our bodies. They help us break down food and detoxify our bodies. However, some microorganisms can be bad actors. One such organism is the hookworm, which lives in the small intestine of humans. Hookworm eggs are highly prolific; they can lay 200,000 eggs in one day. Those eggs need to be released in the soil in order to complete their life cycle. The soil in the eastern Europe, where this medicinal use of feces originated, was often infested with hookworm eggs, and so people living there administered feces-based medicines (usually in the form of a porridge made of feces and laxatives). The medicine was believed to kill hookworms and other intestinal parasites. To me, this is a great example of a purely local and cultural solution to a global problem.

Stealing First Base is an American baseball term that describes the act of taking first base as your one out in an inning. If you are a baserunner, it is important to always remember that the goal is to get to and stay at second base. When a pitcher fails to get a player out, the player's team is "out" and their team's "opponent" is "on base". The "opponent" now has one "out" and one "on base". The "opponent" can substitute a player for the "on base" player. The "out" player can attempt to get a runner "out" by pitching to that runner. If a fielder throws the ball to first baseman before the runner safely reaches second base, the runner is "stealing" first base. This creates a new "opponent" "on base", and the "out" "opponent" is now "at bat" to attempt to get a "hit" or "home run" (a blow-hit) to make the "stealing" runner "safe" at second base. In the above example, the pitcher gave up a hit (a "ball") to the "opponent" first baseman, who fielded the ball and threw it to first base before the runner "stealing" first base could safely reach second base. The runner "stealing" first base is now "safe" at second base and the "opponent" at first base is now "at bat" to attempt to get a "hit" or "home run" (a blow-hit) to make the "stealing" runner "safe" at second base.

Seeking advice from multiple people when making a decision ensures that you are getting a diverse set of viewpoints and experiences. First, diversity in opinions helps create a broader understanding of the situation. In addition, by incorporating different viewpoints, different options become available to you that you may not have considered. For example, one person may have experience with a similar situation, while another person may have an opposing view. Together, these two perspectives give you a wider array of options to consider. Seeking advice from multiple people is a smart strategy for getting a more complete understanding of the situation. Guiding principle: A diverse set of viewpoints generates broader understanding and deeper options Worthy of note: While seeking advice from multiple people can help generate a more complete understanding of the situation, not every situation requires the involvement of multiple people. ellate: Despite the many benefits of seeking advice from multiple people, there are times when the right solution comes from a single person. While it is never a bad idea to seek additional opinions when considering an opportunity, you should only do so when you feel that you will not fully understand the situation or consider all available options unless you do so. Guiding principle: One person can generate a complete understanding of the situation but only at the cost of opportunities for improvement. Worthy of note: While multiple people can generate a more complete understanding of the situation, not every situation lends itself to involving multiple people. When in doubt, it’s better to seek advice from one person in order to avoid burning through all of your reserve fleet resources conducting diverse viewings.

The authors in this paper introduce a framework for training energy-based generative adversarial networks (EB-GANs) and show that it provides a high-fidelity approximation to the data distribution. The paper has a very strong introduction, explaining the motivations and background for this line of research. The authors provide both theoretical analysis and empirical evaluation of the framework. The major weakness of the framework is that it requires several hours to days to train for high-dimensional images, making it inefficient for real-time applications. enabled inference and generation of high-dimensional images? The framework is efficient for high-dimensional images, where the image resolution is large but the dimension (number of pixels) is large. In this case, instead of directly representing the images using a fixed length vector, the authors represent each image using a pixel color code. This dramatically reduces the number of dimensions and thus the model complexity, while enabling the model to still capture the image structure. For low-dimensional images, such as hand-drawn sketches, the model is inefficient and it can take days to train. enables inference and generation of high-dimensional images? Yes. The model is inefficient for low-dimensional images, such as hand-drawn sketches. Instead of directly representing the images using a fixed length vector, the authors represent each image using a pixel color code. This dramatically reduces the number of dimensions and thus the model complexity, while enabling the model to still capture the image structure. How does it compare to normal GANs? The model is more expensive to compute as it needs to compute the per-pixel colors. However, the efficiency of the model only depends on the image dimension but not the dimension of the vector space. Therefore, the inefficiency problem of GANs with per-pixel manipulation is also inherent to the normal GANs. How does it compare to surrogated GANs? The EB-GAN is similar to surrogated GANs, which also uses an energy function to train the generator. However, surrogated GANs uses unphysical equations to represent the generator and this leads to several problems. (1) The training is very unstable and tends to converge to a local minimum instead of global minimum. (2) The spectra of the fake samples is not guaranteed to be constant, which means that a white noise still remains in the sample spectrum. (3) The convergence of the model is slow, and the model cannot produce high-fidelity images in real-time. (4) The training of the model requires days which is too slow for practical usage. It would be interesting to compare the EB-GAN to existing surrogated GANs and analyze the limitations of this type of model. how does it compare to energy-based GANs? The EB-GAN is similar to energy-based GANs. The two models have the same unphysical equations and training process. However, the purpose of the two models is quite different. The energy-based GANs uses unphysical equations to represent the generator, and the objective of the model is to prove that the generator and the discriminator have the same statistical distributions. The two models fail to be reconciled because their objectives are different. The EB-GAN model uses unphysical equations to represent the generator, and the objective is to produce high-fidelity images. The two models can be reconciled by adding new constraints on the model. One possible solution is to assume that the discriminator has a low-fidelity representation of the image and then to minimize the difference between the low-fidelity representation and the generator. how does it compare to prior art? EB-GAN can produce high-fidelity images. The efficiency is still limited by the color representation method for high-dimensional images. The representation method for low-dimensional images is inefficient. how does it compare to state of the art? EB-GAN captures the pixel structures in images, which makes it very efficient for high-dimensional images. However, for low-dimensional images, such as sketches, it is inefficient. how would you optimize this framework? Incorporating efficient representation methods for both low-dimensional and high-dimensional images into the framework. how can this framework be applied to new problems? The framework is designed for images, but it can be easily applied to other types of data. The only constraint is that the data should be able to be represented using a low-dimensional or low-resolution encoding.

Summer break provides an excellent opportunity for students to continue to learn new concepts and practice subject matter in a different context than school. Creating summer projects that require students to apply what they have learned over the course of the school year can help ensure they maintain the knowledge required for success. However, summer is often a low stakes environment in which it is easy for students to take time to complete assignments or take shortcuts that result in incorrect answers. For this reason, requiring students to solve real-world problems rather than mere exercises can be a better way to ensure that students maintain their knowledge and understanding over the summer. Sun break provides an excellent opportunity for students to continue to learn new concepts and practice subject matter in a different context than school. Creating summer projects that require students to apply what they have learned over the course of the school year can help ensure they maintain the knowledge required for success. However, summer is often a low stakes environment in which it is easy for students to take time to complete assignments or take shortcuts that result in incorrect answers. For this reason, requiring students to solve real-world problems rather than mere exercises creates a higher-barrier to entry that increases the likelihood that students will actually complete the projects and ensure they do not learn only how to complete the task, but also apply what they have learned. Shushing people became an acceptable way to tell others to be quiet sometime in the 14th century. This might come as a surprise to many, especially those living in modern times, but throughout human history being quiet in public places was difficult and often dangerous. Because of this, shushing was and still is a very effective way of keeping quiet in public places. There are several theories about when shushing first became popular. One popular theory suggests that because of the prevalence of plague disease in the 14th century, people began to associate loud noises with the plague and wanted to avoid making any noises that might be contagious. Another theory suggests that the more industrialized and modern life became during the 19th century, the more people tended to talk with loud voices in public and thus shushing became more common. Finally, some suggest that shushing became popular due to the increased use of headphones among listeners in modern times, as the shushing sound is louder and can be heard over outside noises, even though the introduction of headphones was not widespread until much later.

The Neural Belief Tracking (NBT) framework proposed in this paper addresses the problems of previous belief trackers by relying on pre-trained word vectors. Instead of hand-crafted lexicons, NBT models reason over pre-trained word vectors, learning to compose them into distributed representations of user utterances and dialogue context. We conduct experiments on two public datasets, and the results show that NBT significantly outperforms previous belief trackers. The main contributions of this paper are as follows: 1. a novel neural belief tracker is proposed which relies on representation learning to overcome the shortcomings of previous belief trackers; 2. a new public dataset is created to evaluate belief trackers, which can be accessed at https://github.com/xiaolong1566/NBT-Dataset. Some of the advantages of using representation learning for belief tracking are as follows: 1. The requirement of lexicon in previous belief trackers limits the application of belief trackers to smaller domains, where the necessary lexicon can be compiled; 2. Most previous belief trackers only use a small number of pre-defined lexicon categories, which may not fully capture the variations in users' language; 3. The modeling of dialogue language from a distributed semantic representation allows NBT to generalize beyond the specific labeled data used to train it. For example, NBT outperformed prior work when no labeled data were available for training, indicating that NBT has greater generalizability. One of the main contributions of this work is to release a new public dataset for belief tracking, referred to as NBT-Dataset. This dataset is created based on the CHAQ-10 corpus, which is widely used in prior work. However, compared with other similar datasets, NBT-Dataset has the following three main differences: 1. it allows for self-taught pre-training of word vectors on out-of-domain data; 2. it includes more fine-grained annotation of dialogue acts and relaxed coreference resolution; 3. it includes dialogue data in multiple languages, including English, Spanish and Chinese. This dataset is suitable for research using both supervised and unsupervised learning techniques for belief tracking, and can be downloaded from https://github.com/xiaolong1566/NBT-Dataset. In summary, this paper makes the following significant contributions: 1. It advances the state of the art in belief tracking by developing a representation learning approach; 2. It creates the NBT-Dataset, a public dataset for belief tracking, which is suitable for research using both supervised and unsupervised learning techniques for belief tracking; 3. It makes the pre-trained word vector technology available to the dialogue community, making it easier to integrate into existing belief tracking systems. The main contributions of this paper are as follows: 1. A representation learning approach for belief tracking is proposed which relies on pre-trained word vectors; 2. A new public dataset is created to evaluate belief trackers, which can be accessed at https://github.com/xiaolong1566/NBT-Dataset. Some of the advantages of using representation learning for belief tracking are as follows: 1. The reliance on pre-trained word vectors has the advantage of flexibility, making it possible to apply the model to domains where labeled data are scarce or non-existent; 2. The use of pre-trained word vectors allows the tracker to be trained from unlabeled data as well as labeled, providing the model with the ability to learn from unlabeled data; 3. The representation learning approach can help eliminate the need for hand-crafted lexicons and allow the model to learn dialogue language from distributed semantic representations. Diverse Beam Search (DBS) is an algorithm that uses reinforcement learning to train a model to dynamically change the subset of beams it searches through according to the characteristics of the data being trained on. By using a diverse ensemble of explore and exploit strategies, DBS is able to better explore the search space and achieve better final performance on the task at hand. DBS is especially powerful when the underlying reinforcement learning algorithm is not easily able to find good solutions, such as when using neural networks for search and Brockauls exploration strategy. This paper builds off of DBS by proposing an algorithm for using reinforcement learning to generate diverse solutions to the problem of sequence modeling. The algorithm, Diverse Beam Search: Decoding Diverse Solutions (DBS-DS), builds on the original DBS algorithm by training a sequence modeling agent on a set of diverse solutions, instead of a single'elites' sequence as used in the original DBS paper. The proposed method is shown to achieve better results than the original DBS algorithm on multiple datasets, including the original CIFAR10 and the more difficult COCO10 datasets. This paper makes the following contributions: 1. A method for using reinforcement learning for sequence modeling to generate diverse solutions 2. A comparison of DBS and DBS-DS using CIFAR10 and COCO10 datasets 3. A visualization method for determining the diversity of the generated solutions This paper makes the following contributions: 1. A method for using reinforcement learning for sequence modeling to generate diverse solutions 2. A comparison of DBS and DBS-DS using CIFAR10 and COCO10 datasets 3. A visualization method for determining the diversity of the generated solutions The original DBS algorithm generates a single sequence of solution actions, the elite sequence, from which it samples a single solution. The proposed DBS-DS algorithm uses a set of diverse solutions generated by reinforcement learning to provide a more diverse ensemble for sampling from. By using a range of exploratory strategies, such as a varying number of samples from different time periods, DBS-DS is able to achieve better performance than the original DBS algorithm on some tasks. The improvements achieved by the original DBS algorithm are also extended to the DBS-DS algorithm through the development of a new exploration strategy, Brockauls, which uses the logged quality of each sequence to determine the exploration rate of the policy. The original CIFAR10 dataset has been one of the most common datasets for benchmarking sequence modeling algorithms. It has a simple, well understood goal, handwritten digit classification, and can be quickly trained on even large datasets. The CIFAR10 dataset is also not well suited to the explored approach of the original DBS algorithm, as it struggles with small, but significant, shifts in the input data. DBS-DS shows promise on this dataset, and if the improvements of DBS-DS can be applied to a better explored approach to DBS, it could yield even better results. The COCO10 dataset is a more recent and challenging dataset, designed to test model performance on more complex scenarios. Unlike CIFAR10, it exposes the model to more significant changes in the input data. DBS-DS shows significantly more promise on this dataset than the original DBS algorithm, likely due to the more varied search strategy and effective exploration technique. In summary, DBS-DS is an effective way of combining the benefits of the original DBS algorithm with the improved exploration techniques of DBS-DS. Car use in our society has significantly improved over the years with increased fuel efficiency and reduced emissions. However, we are still relying heavily on cars, with over 95% of commutes being by car. Many major cities around the world are suffering from severe air pollution, with thousands of deaths each year as a result. Many environmental groups have called on companies to reduce their reliance on private vehicles by supporting carpooling and other forms of sustainable transportation. Car-Free Day, which started in Bogota, Colombia in 2016, has spread to other cities and is now supported by citizens of over 70 cities around the world. Other organizations, such as Transportation Alternatives, have been successful in pushing companies to reduce car use. The 'Paris is Cars' movement, where citizens protest against congestion by Juggernauts (massive round-about clogged with hundreds of vehicles) have dramatically reduced the number of vehicles on the road. Eliminating the use of private cars would allow more cities to invest in mass transit, which would improve the air quality, drastically increase ridership, and create a larger pedestrian friendly space. The return on investment would be tremendous - the public transport network in a city like Singapore, which only has a population of around 20 million, is one of the densest in the world and runs mostly on buses. Similar networks could be developed for other cities around the world. The switch to electric vehicles, as opposed to internal combustion engines, would provide a significant improvement in terms of emissions and the energy independence of the country. By 2050, the UN has estimated that 56% of vehicles in the United States will be electric. Ultimately, eliminating the personal vehicle would remove the biggest source of stress for most people - the need to find parking. Most cities already restrict the number of parking spots for cars to encourage alternatives like public transportation and ride-sharing. The adoption of these policies and an overall reduction in vehicle ownership could significantly reduce greenhouse gas emissions and improve the quality of life for citizens.

The inner magnetosphere plasma pressure distribution around the planet was obtained using satellite measurements from Geosynchronous satellite (GSO) and polar satellites during the March 1-8, 1982 geomagnetic storm. The radial distribution shows that pressure was depleted from approximately 8 R_E (earth radii) to 4 R_E during the storm. There was no radial pressure enhancement noted during the storm. The depletion of pressure began at around 6 R_E and extended to 3 R_E during the night of March 2nd. This pressure depletion coincided with a period of intense negative Bz in the midlatitude regions, and can be explained by adiabatic expansion of plasma in magnetic fields. However, the pressure depletion during the storm was more extensive than that predicted by the adiabatic expansion model. Additional depletion may have been caused by interaction of the storm plasma with the ring current. The depletion of pressure began at around 6 R_E and extended to 3 R_E during the night of March 2nd. This pressure depletion coincided with a period of intense negative Bz in the midlatitude regions, and can be explained by adiabatic expansion of plasma in magnetic fields. However, the pressure depletion during the storm was more extensive than that predicted by the adiabatic expansion model. Additional depletion may have been caused by interaction of the storm plasma with the ring current. The depletion of pressure began at a distance of 6 R_E, coinciding with the radial distance at which ring current end effects begin to become important. The depletion reached a maximum of 3 R_E, approximately the distance at which the perturbation in the ring current become maximum.

Load your 35mm film into your manual camera - before you head out for the day! Decide what lighting conditions will be present, pick up your camera and... 1. Find the circular disc that is on top that has an arrow drawn on the disc. 2. Look to the left of the top of the camera. 3. Find the circular piece that has an arrow drawn on it. 4. Look to the left of this circular piece. 5. Find the small black knob. 6. Slide the knob towards you and as you do this, pull up on the circular disc. 7. Pull gently but firmly up on the disc until you hear the back door of the camera pop open. 8. If you were unable to open the back door, push the circular disc back down and repeat. 9. Unpackage your roll of film. 10. Take the roll of film and lay it on top of the cradle (not in) on the left hand side. 11. Pull up on the same disc from before as you do this. 12. Next, with the roll of film securely in place, pull the leader across the back of the camera by grabbing the sides of the film so no fingerprints make it on the surface of the film. 13. Once the film has reached the other side of the camera, you must lock the film into the winding mechanism so that the film will move across the film plane when it is winded after every exposure. 14. Insert the leader into one of the two slits on the right spindle. 15. Line up the sprocket holes on the sprockets. 16. Holding the film in place on the sprockets and with the leader in a slit on the spindle, with your left hand, find the advancement mechanism which is connected to the right spindle but on top of the camera and spin it out and to the right with your right thumb. 17. Repeat previous steps three to four times to ensure that the film is firmly connected to the spindle. 18. Shut the back door. 19. Repeat two more times.

I think this paper does a great job addressing a question that many people have been wondering: how can we identify American jazz musicians from the 1950s? The paper provides an innovative way to do this by extracting data from Zillow, a real estate website, and natural language processing (NLP) to identify musician-related text in the Zillow description of homes sold during that time period. This is a great approach because many jazz musicians made their living performing in night clubs and their homes often provided the local meeting place for these performances. This approach has several advantages over previous ways to identify jazz musicians. First, it does not rely on hand-entered data, so that anyone can apply this approach to new data and new time periods. Second, it overcomes the problems with previous hand-entered approaches by using NLP to automatically extract musician-related text from Zillow home descriptions, and then using that text to train a machine learning model to identify Jazz musicians. This approach is more accurate than previous jazz musician identification approaches, because it does not rely on artist listings or biographical dictionaries, which are difficult to update and therefore do not reflect current musicians. Limitations of this approach include the lack of detail in home descriptions (e.g. no mention of "head" or "mic"), as well as the home-bias, which means that the most compelling jazz musicians, who often perform with other musicians, will have their homes sold more frequently. I recommend this paper for publication in JASIST!

Saccopharynx lavenbergi is a species of sea snail, a marine gastropod mollusk in the family Clavatulidae. The species was named after Australian malacologist David Lavenberg. Mannert van Altena originally named this species as Microseptaphryn lavenbergi, however the specific name honours the malacologist David Lavenberg. Saccopharynx lavenbergi is the only known species in the genus Saccopharynx. The species was discovered by Dr Lavenberg and his research student, Van Altena, during a cruise off the northern west coast of Australia in August 2010. It lives near the bottom in deep water, about two hundred meters down. It feeds on trace metals released by the sediments, and will not grow if brought to the surface. Destroying things like screens protectors or bottles may cause the person to believe that they can destroy something else in order to remove the source of stress. People who suffer from depression may also destroy objects as a way to prevent thoughts of suicide. There are treatments for mental health issues like counseling or therapy, as well as self-help methods like creating a detailed to-do list or taking a walk outside. The person's psychiatrist or mental health professional can suggest the best method for helping the person with mental health issues. Even if you aren't experiencing any symptoms, it is important to seek help if you ever have questions about your mental health. If you notice any of the symptoms described here, please see a professional. Mental health is important, and no one should suffer from it silently. Have you ever had that feeling when you get home, finish your homework, maybe hangout with some friends and your still bored? Well, an extracurricular activity like a sport or a club would really be great for you! I think that every student should do at least one extracurricular activity it would help students make new friends students could earn scholarships while learning on the way.
 Doing something else around the community would really get yourself out there to make new friends and open up new opportunities. This would help you later in life when you might need to make friends in college, and those friends would help you out before your exams and other times like that. All in all this is just something everybody should do. Most clubs through the school are free so I would sign up now to find new friends, open new opportunities, and even earn a scholarship to a college.

Yuval Cherlow (born 1957) is a Modern Orthodox rabbi and posek. He is Rosh Yeshiva of Yeshivat Hesder Amit Orot Shaul in Tel Aviv, Israel. Cherlow was one of the founders of Tzohar, an organization of religious Zionist Orthodox rabbis in Israel. Yuval Cherlow was born in Herzliya, Israel. His parents were American-Jewish immigrants who moved to Israel in 1949. He was educated in Yeshivat Har Etzion. As part of his Hesder studies, he served as an armor officer in the IDF. In the reserves he was a company commander in the armoured corps. He lives in Efrat with his wife, who is a lecturer in the department of Jewish philosophy in Bar Ilan University. Cherlow's rabbinic career began in Kibbutz Tirat Tzvi, where he served as a rabbi and teacher. Later he served as a rabbi in Yeshivat HaGolan in Hispin, in the southern Golan Heights. After the Rabin assassination, he helped found Tzohar, whose aim was to lessen the tensions between religious and non-religious communities. In 1998 he founded a Yeshivat Hesder in Petach Tikva, later to become Yeshivat Hesder Amit Orot Shaul in Kfar Batya, Raanana. He justified his decision to start a new yeshiva in the central area of Israel by saying that it is the place where the most important debates about the identity of the country take place. Since 2001 Cherlow has answered more than 30,000 questions about Halakha and other issues related to Judaism via the Moreshet website. He published two books based on his internet responsa. He has also published books that deal with Bible commentary as well as the writings of Rabbi Avraham Yitzhak Kook. Cherlow is a member of the ethics committee of the Israeli Ministry of Health and the committee which allocates the budget for new drugs. He is active in the BeMaagalei Tzedek ("Circles of Justice") movement. In 2011, he took part in the Israeli social justice protests. Cherlow has voiced empathy for the difficulties faced by religious homosexual people, and has called for the Orthodox community to treat them with compassion. However, he still maintains that homosexual relationships are prohibited by Halakha. Cherlow has voiced liberal positions on some issues, such as allowing the use of artificial insemination by unmarried women in certain circumstances and supporting coed activities in Bnei Akiva. His view on Jewish marriage in Israel is that all Jews should be married according to Halakha, but if the Chief Rabbinate refuses to marry a couple, there should be an alternate method of being recognized as a couple according to secular law. He is opposed to civil disobedience, an issue that rose to the fore during Israel's unilateral disengagement from Gaza and evacuation of Israeli settlements in the region. In 2012, Cherlow called on the State of Israel to recognise non-Orthodox streams of Judaism and Reform conversions. In response, Tzohar distanced itself from his remarks. Yizhar Hess of the Masorti Movement in Israel has suggested that Cherlow does not represent the mainstream of Zionist Orthodoxy in Israel, and Rabbi Yehoshua Shapira described Cherlow as a "neo-Reform rabbi in the Orthodox sector." Cherlow is also interested in interfaith dialogue, and currently sits on the Board of World Religious Leaders for the Elijah Interfaith Institute. Rabbi Cherlow deals mainly with ethical issues. He writes articles and tweets about ethics in Israel. In 2015, he established the ethics department at Tzohar. His book "BeTzalmo" (In His Image), published in 2015 by Maggid Press, deals with the religious meanings of God's image in people. In March of 2018 he garnered attention for stating that genetically cloned pig or any other animal would be Kosher.

The Woolmer lecture is the flagship lecture of the Institute of Physics and Engineering in Medicine. It takes place annually during the Institute's Medical Physics and Engineering Conference. The lecture is dedicated to Professor Ronald Woolmer who was the first Director of the Research Department of Anaesthetics at the Royal College of Surgeons. Woolmer convened a meeting at the Royal College of Surgeons, London, to discuss the evolving field of engineering applied to medicine. It was agreed that the group should hold regular meetings and as a result the Biological Engineering Society (BES) was formed with Ronald Woolmer as the first President. Woolmer died two years after the formation of the BES and it was agreed that a memorial lecture would be sponsored in recognition of his achievements.

An extensive literature exists describing various techniques for theevaluation of Hankel determinants. The prevailing methods such as Dodgsoncondensation, continued fraction expansion, LU decomposition, all produceproduct formulas when they are applicable. We mention the classic case of theHankel determinants with binomial entries ${3k+2 \choose k}$ and those withentries ${3k \choose k}$; both of these classes of Hankel determinants haveproduct form evaluations. The intermediate case, ${3k+1 \choose k}$ has notbeen evaluated. There is a good reason for this: these latter determinants donot have product form evaluations. In this paper we evaluate the Hankel determinant of ${3k+1 \choose k}$. Theevaluation is a sum of a small number of products, an almost product. The method actually provides more, and as applications, we present thesalient points for the evaluation of a number of other Hankel determinants withpolynomial entries, along with product and almost product form evaluations atspecial points.

Are you asking about what happened *in* surf culture during the 70s, 80s & 90s, or are you making an assumption that surf culture somehow vanished following the 60s?
The brief answer to the second is that it didn't: witness [*Surfer* magazine](_URL_4_), which as far as I know has been continually published since 1960 and is regarded as the surfer's bible. Surfing was one of the first really recognizable parts of the counterculture (R.Crumb did subscription ads for *Surfer* in the late 60s), and unlike a lot of West Coast hippie culture, was more or less actively embraced in the early punk scene, half ironically and half not. (Witness [JFA's "Beach Blanket Bongout"](_URL_1_) or, heck, that reverb-drenched guitar in ["Holiday in Cambodia"](_URL_3_), straight out of the Surfaris.)
You can check out [Matt Warshaw's *History of Surfing*](_URL_0_) or [*Encyclopedia of Surfing*](_URL_2_) (which has an awesome online presence) for more on the how and where and why surfing went through the changes it did, both as a sport and as a lifestyle. 
I mean, you could go into the difference between "surf rock" and "surf punk" and the actual activity of surfing, or the evolution of board styles (the mid-70s brought in shortboards with thruster skeg configuration, which made the hot-dogging of the 1980s possible, followed by longboard nostalgia of the 90s and 00s), but it kind of depends on where you want to go....

(Note: the 8’ board will be the base of our structure and the 6’ will be the vertical beam.)
Then screw them in using 2 screws for each point of contact on the base and only one screw each into the vertical board.
This should result in both support structures to be parallel. With the second 2’8” board screw it to the posterior end of the two supports.
Drill a hole in the inside of the vertical boards, with thickness articulating to the bolt width and about 1” deep. These will act as grooves for our 1/2 threaded bolts.
If your lens differs in size, that’s okay just find the midpoint to it’s easy to balance.
Use Spacers to get the Fresnel Lens a taught feel, but not too taught that it’s fully immobilized.

Carefully move the box slowly to disperse the focal point into a design for the first layer.
Layers of sand should be added at about 1/8” per level. By sprinkling the sand is small stages this will allow for more preciseness of the levels of melted silicon(sand) on top of itself.

The space velocities from the catalog of Nordstrom et al. (2004) are used totrace variations of a number of kinematic parameters of single F and G dwarfsas a function of their age. The vertex deviation of disk stars increases from7+- 1 to 15+-2 degrees as the mean age decreases from 4.3 to 1.5 Gyr. Thetwo-dimensional velocity distributions in the UV, UW, and VW planes areanalyzed. The evolution of the main peaks in the velocity distributions can befollowed to an average age of approximately 9 Gyr. We find that: (1) in thedistributions of the UV velocity components, stars of different types areconcentrated toward several stable peaks (the Hyades, Pleiades, and SiriusCluster), suggesting that the stars belonging to these formations did not formsimultaneously; (2) the peak associated with the Hyades Cluster dominates inall age intervals; and (3) the Hyades peak is strongest for stars with anaverage age of 1.5 Gyr, suggesting that this peak contains a considerablefraction of stars from the Hyades cluster. The age dependences of the kinematicparameters exhibit a break near 4.5 Gyr, which can be explained as an effect ofthe different contributions of stars of the thin and thick disks. The Strombergrelation yields a solar LSR velocity of V_{\odot LSR} = (8.7, 6.2, 7.2)+- (0.5,2.2, 0.8) km/s.

Physical abuse may be easy to spot if the child has no way of concealing the injury, but there are also some more subtle signs. Common symptoms of physical abuse include:Having injuries such as bruises, cuts, or burns that the child either cannot or will not explain
Having injuries that are patterned (such as looking like the injury is from a belt)
Acting anxious or always on guard
Flinching at sudden touch or movement, and may be averse to being touched
Showing signs of discomfort when movingWearing inappropriate clothing to cover arms and legs, such as long sleeves on hot days;
Emotional abuse can be difficult to spot, as it doesn't leave physical markings; however, it is extremely important to keep an eye out for. Some symptoms of emotional abuse include:Having low self-esteem or self-confidence
Seeking affection from others
Being distrustful or fearful
Having trouble with emotional regulationStruggling with social interactionActing older or younger than is appropriate for the child’s age, such as thumb sucking or taking on care of other children, Sexual abuse can cause severe emotional problems for a child that may last for his or her lifetime. Children who are sexually abused often feel like the abuse is their fault, and many children are ashamed to come forward.Some signs of sexual abuse include:Sexual knowledge or actions that don't match the child's age group
Trouble walking or sitting down
Unwillingness to change clothing in front of others
Fear of certain places or peoplePregnancy in adolescent females
Neglect is when a child’s basic needs are not being met, such as food, clothing, hygiene, and affection. Neglect can be difficult to notice, or it can be obvious. Signs of neglect may include:Consistent uncleanliness, including body odor
Lack of weather-appropriate clothing
Requests for food or other necessities, and hoarding these when possible
Being underweight or malnourished
Having untreated physical or psychological problems, Most children exhibit behavioral changes under stress, so it's a good idea to look for changes in behavior when attempting to identify a victim of child abuse. However, keep in mind that these symptoms can apply to a child under stress that isn't related to abuse; don't assume that just because you see these symptoms, they're being abused.
In older children, you may see behavioral regression. Behavioral regression is when a child reverts back to old behaviors that they did when they were younger. Examples include thumb-sucking, bedwetting, and emotional problems such as throwing tantrums.
Notice changes in eating habits. Does the child eat much more than he or she used to, or does the child seem less interested in food? Is the child gaining or losing weight?
Is the child scared of a certain person or group of people? It's normal for children to be afraid of some people, such as those who may look frightening, but if the child seems to be scared of an older child, a group of children, an adult, or a group of adults, then the child may be being abused.
Is the child vandalizing property or stealing? Sometimes, children may be stealing out of necessity, such as if they are being neglected. Pay attention to what the child takes because taking food or money can be a sign of neglect., If the child loses friends rapidly, withdraws from social activities, suddenly closes off from trusted friends, or begins spending all their time with an older child or adult, these may be indicators that the child is being abused. Keep an eye out for aggressive behavior as well. Children who are being abused may start fights, whether verbal or physical.Try to find out why the child's friends are no longer spending time with the child. If the friends describe the child as becoming secretive, moody, or devoting all of his or her time to another person, take a closer look at the situation.
If the child didn't have many friends to begin with, watch for signs of bullying. Bullying can contribute to symptoms of abuse, but the child may be being abused as well.
A child who is being abused can become anxious, jumpy, depressed, angry, or show a combination of emotions.Children who have been emotionally abused may also have trouble controlling their emotions and have large breakdowns or emotional outbursts that may frighten other children.
The child’s grades may also drop. However, don't assume that a child with great grades isn't having home trouble. A child can be abused and still keep up with schoolwork - and keeping up with schoolwork may be forced by the abuser.
In some cases of child abuse, the child may resort to self-destructive behaviors or may even become suicidal.If the child has admitted to you that he or she engages in self-destructive behaviors, seek help for the child immediately. Self-destructive behaviors include:
Self-injury, such as cutting or burning
Suicidal thoughts or intentions
Drug or alcohol abuse
Intents or threats to harm others
If you know an adult who you suspect has been abused during childhood, recognize that most adults are not willing to talk about it. An adult who has been abused during childhood may be secretive about his or her past. It will be necessary to take a closer look at the adult in question unless he or she trusts you enough to tell you.
Child abuse has been linked to increased risk of physical health problems, though it is uncertain why this is.Adults who were abused in childhood are at a higher risk of developing heart disease, diabetes, liver problems, hepatitis, arthritis, and more.Be aware, however, that physical health problems can occur in people who haven't been abused, so it shouldn't be your sole piece of evidence.
Don't ignore smaller problems such as headaches or stomach pain. While these may seem too common to be a symptom of past abuse, if they are occurring frequently, it may be the body's stress response.
Victims of child abuse may have trouble regulating or feeling their emotions.They may develop or suffer from mental illnesses such as depression, anxiety, post-traumatic stress disorder (PTSD), dissociative disorders, and personality disorders.Research has shown that children who are abused are more likely to develop depression or PTSD than children who weren't abused.
Adults who were victims of child abuse are more likely to engage in self-injurious behaviors or attempt suicide than those who weren't.Eating disorders can also be a sign of an abusive past.
Be aware that most people aren't going to open up about their mental health to you, especially if you don't know the person well.
Do not confront this person over his or her behavior unless you have reason to believe that he or she is suicidal. If the person is showing symptoms of depression, though, you may still want to reach out and offer your support. Even if you have reason to believe that this person is suicidal, be careful about how you talk to them about it.
Victims of child abuse are more likely to engage in violent and criminal behavior than those who are not victims.If this person has a history of cruelty towards people, animals, getting arrested, and abusive behavior towards their partner or spouse, they may have been a victim of child abuse.
Child abuse has also been linked to development of personality disorders, such as antisocial personality disorder.However, it is important to remember that someone's past does not justify their current actions; it merely serves as an explanation for why the person has certain behaviors.
Keep in mind, however, that just because someone rejects social advances does not necessarily mean he or she was abused. Other mental illnesses, such as depression and anxiety, can cause a person to go into social isolation.
Abuse has been proven to cause abnormal stress responses - more specifically, the level of cortisol released in the brain is less than usual.Victims of child abuse can develop symptoms of post-traumatic stress disorder, or even PTSD itself; one of these symptoms is hyperarousal, or fight-or-flight mode. Symptoms of hyperarousal include:Sleep disturbances, such as insomnia or nightmares - the affected person in question may be constantly tired
Irritability, anger, or aggression
Impulsiveness
Constantly feeling unsafe
While you can't go back in time and get the person out of their past abusive situation, you may be able to help the person recover in adulthood or intervene if the person is suicidal. Keep an eye out for any signs of suicidal intent, abuse of alcohol or drugs, extreme self-injurious or reckless behavior, or violence towards other people.
If the adult who is a victim of child abuse has stated that he or she intends to commit suicide or harm others, contact emergency services immediately and try to distract the person until help arrives. Avoid leaving the person alone.
The term child abuse is often used to cover many different types of abuse. In order to truly identify a victim of child abuse, you will need to understand the types of child abuse and where it comes from.Physical abuse is abuse involving physical actions such as slapping, kicking, pushing, hair-pulling, choking, and more.
Emotional abuse is abuse involving emotional manipulation, threats, and put-downs - for example, a parent telling his or her child that the child is worthless and threatening to kick the child out.
Sexual abuse is abuse of a sexual nature, such as rape, sexual touching, or taking pornographic photos of the child.
Neglect is abuse involving a lack of care for the child. A neglected child may have poor hygiene and ill-fitting clothing and seem to be constantly unsupervised.
To identify a child that has been abused, you might have to dispel certain misconceptions about child abuse. Common misconceptions include:Child abuse is only violent or Physical abuse is more severe: Victims of child abuse who were emotionally abused or neglected can carry the memories of abuse for as long as those who are physically abused. No form of abuse is "worse" than another form.
Children lie about being abused: Many statistics, such as police and court statistics, show that it is very rare for children to claim that they have been abused when they haven't. It has also been found that it is more common for children to say that they aren't being abused when they really are.They did something to deserve it: While parents do have to set rules and sometimes enforce them for their child, a parent who abuses his or her child is the one at fault, not the child. Even if the child has been severely misbehaving, the parent's job is not to hit, threaten, or belittle them. A child who has been dressing "older than his or her age" isn't responsible for sexual abuse, a child who has been misbehaving isn't responsible for physical abuse, and a child who is emotional towards their parents isn't responsible for emotional abuse.
Disabled children aren't at risk for abuse: Researchers have proved that disabled children are actually at a higher risk for abuse than children without disabilities.For this reason, it's a good idea to keep an eye on disabled children and watch for signs that they may be being abused.
Children who are abused should be removed from the home: In cases of child abuse, the child is only removed from the home by child protective services if keeping the child at home would be dangerous, as removing a child from his or her home can distress the child. Most of the time, the child is kept at home while outside help is provided for the family.
Sexual abuse is done by strangers: Sexual abuse of a child is not limited to strangers. Most children who are sexually abused are abused by people that they know.
Children who are abused are often found to suffer from adverse effects, such as increased risk of substance abuse, physical health problems, mental health problems, and problems with interpersonal relationships.If you suspect a child is being abused, do not blow off the situation; child abuse has been proven to cause problems that can extend well into adulthood.
Children who have been abused have a heightened risk of self-harming behaviors as well as suicidal thinking. Other effects of child abuse are depression, anxiety, post-traumatic stress disorder (PTSD), personality disorders, eating disorders, and dissociative disorders.Not getting help for an abused child can result in severe problems for the child later on in life.

Before the conquistadors arrived from Spain, most individuals in the Aztec empire lived in small communities under the jurisdiction of a local ruler. The ruler (*tlatonai*) was entitled to tribute and service from those he ruled. When the Spanish arrived, they continued the concept of a *tlatonai* because of its convenience. 
While the Aztecs permanently lost control of their empire after the Spanish Administration of 1521, natives were allowed to serve in the government. The chief political institution in conquered towns was the *cabildo*, which was always staffed by natives. Officials were democratically elected by the local native populations.
The Spanish employed a legal system known as the *encomienda* in which certain Aztec natives (loyal to colonists) were assigned to collect tribute and to force other natives to work for the Spanish. The *encomienda* system was sold as an opportunity to teach natives the Spanish language and the Catholic faith, but in practice, it was equivalent to slavery. However, slavery was illegal under the Spanish crown and the Aztecs were considered subjects of Spain. The loop-hole that permitted the *encomienda* system was that native populations were not technically slaves because they were free and not regarded as property.
Traditionally, Aztec culture embraced mass labor and even considered it rewarding as a shared experience. The Spanish seized this opportunity and exploited the native work ethic and, under the system of *encomienda*, the natives were reduced to slaves in all but name.
Hope this answers part of your question. For more information, check out Charles Gibson's *The Aztecs Under Spanish Rule*. (Stanford University Press, 1964). Gibson describes in great detail the deterioration of the Aztec civilization during the process of Hispanization and the subsequent loss of the Aztec empire.

We report an Atomic Force Microscopy (AFM) study on thick multi lamellarstacks of approx. 10 mum thickness (about 1500 stacked membranes) of DMPC(1,2-dimyristoyl-sn-glycero-3-phoshatidylcholine) deposited on silicon wafers.These thick stacks could be stabilized for measurements under excess water orsolution. From force curves we determine the compressional modulus B and therupture force F_r of the bilayers in the gel (ripple), the fluid phase and inthe range of critical swelling close to the main transition. AFM allows tomeasure the compressional modulus of stacked membrane systems and values for Bcompare well to values reported in the literature. We observe pronouncedripples on the top layer in the Pbeta' (ripple) phase and find an increasingripple period Lambda_r when approaching the temperature of the main phasetransition into the fluid Lalpha phase at about 24 C. Metastable ripples with2Lambda_r are observed. Lambda_r also increases with increasing osmoticpressure, i.e., for different concentrations of polyethylene glycol (PEG).

The "winner takes all" system we've been using doesn't work. Less power is essentially being given to the people, some are even being ignored.
 In cases such as Al Gore's, the people said yes, and the College said no. And, voters didn't like this. According to a Gallup poll in 2000, after Al Gore lost the race, "over 60% of voters would prefer a direct election to the kind we have now", meaning, Mr. Senator, it's time for change. In addition, this system easily allows for a tie. In this event, the results of the race would be decided by the House of Representatives, which we all know would swiftly put in a vote for the Republican canidate. And, since the Senate has also been recently dominated by Republicans, they too would choose one of their own, resulting in two branches of the government dominated by one political party. 
Moreover, with this faulty system, many people are being ignored. As you may recall (or not), "During the 2000 campaign, seventeen states didn't see the canidates at all, including Rhode Island and South Carolina, and voters in 25 of the largest media markets didn't get to see a single campaign ad."(Source 2, Paragraph 13) [(Although not seeing the ads might not have been a bad thing. (I'm just kidding.)] Why is this important? Because the people need to see and meet their canidates, but due to the electoral collage, people are being ignored. If the majority of the votes are known to be going to one canidate or the other, they will most likely not be visited by either side, because whom that state's electoral votes are going to are essentially locked in. The only states that recieve any attention are the "Swing states" (Source 2, Paragraph 13) that are pretty equal on both sides and can be easily swayed to pick one canidate or the other. The only evidence to refute my claim would be that "Maine and Nebraska each have a variation of proportional representation" (Source 1, Paragraph 7), which actually might work; however, this would require all the states to change their policies, which we all know won't happen very easily. So, in summary, Mr. Senator, the way things work is going to need to change, we are being ignored and elections are being thrown. If you don't help us change this, the power of electing the president is going to remain with our, faulty, college.

Pores are one of the various features forming in the photosphere by theemergence of magnetic field onto the solar surface. They lie at the borderbetween tiny magnetic elements and larger sunspots. Light bridges, in suchstructures, are bright features separating umbral areas in two or moreirregular regions. Commonly, light bridges indicate that a the merging ofmagnetic regions or, conversely, the breakup of the area is underway. Weinvestigate the velocity structure of a solar pore (AR10812) with light bridge,and of the quiet solar photosphere nearby, analyzing high spatial and spectralresolution images. The pore area has been observed with the InterferometricBI-dimensional Spectrometer (IBIS) at the Dunn Solar Telescope, acquiringmonochromatic images in the Ca II 854.2 nm line and in the Fe I 709.0 nm lineas well as G-band and broad-band images. We also computed the Line of Sight(LoS) velocity field associated to the Fe I and Fe II photospheric lines. Theamplitude of the LoS velocity fluctuations, inside the pore, is smaller thanthat observed in the quiet granulation near the active region. We computed theazimuthal average LoS velocity and derived its radial profile. The whole poreis characterized by a downward velocity -200 m/s and by an annular downflowstructure with an average velocity of -350 m/s with respect to the nearby quietsun. The light bridge inside the pore, when observed in the broad-band channelof IBIS and in the red wing of Ca II 854.2 nm line, shows an elongated darkstructure running along its axis, that we explain with a semi-analytical model.In the highest resolution LoS velocity images the light bridge shows a profileconsistent with a convective roll: a weak upflow, 50-100m/s, in correspondenceof the dark lane, flanked by a downflow, -(200-300) m/s.
